methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $110M - $200M.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

private.name: Graphyte
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $200M

private.name: Fourth Power
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $150M

private.name: Steady Energy
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $110M

data.narrative:
Carbyon is a Series A climate and energy company that has raised €22.3M to date. The valuation analysis draws from a comparable universe of three same-stage, same-sector peers in Climate & Energy, supplemented by a broader set of eight private companies and one public company (LNZA) for market context.

The peer-median method anchors the analysis. The three direct comparables—Graphyte ($200M), Fourth Power ($150M), and Steady Energy ($110M)—yield a median valuation of $150M, with a range spanning $110M to $200M. This method is the applicable triangulation approach given data availability. No revenue or EBITDA multiples were supplied, precluding DCF or comparable-company multiple approaches at this stage.

The triangulated valuation range is $128M (low) to $172M (high), centered on $150M. This reflects the peer-median result with modest sensitivity applied. Key uncertainties include the limited sample size of three comparables, which may not fully capture Carbyon's specific technology differentiation or unit economics relative to peers. The lack of revenue or profitability metrics prevents ratio-based validation of the peer multiples. Investors should treat the $150M midpoint as indicative rather than precise, with meaningful variance possible pending disclosure of technology maturity and path to revenue generation.
